PROJECT SUMMARY

SESI Consulting Engineers (SESI), at the request of Criterion Group, LLC (User) has conducted
a Phase I Environmental Site Assessment (ESA) for the subject property located at 76-84 Central
Avenue (identified as 76 Central Avenue), Kearney, Hudson County, New Jersey (herein referred
to as the “Site”). The Site is identified on local tax maps as Block 288 Lots 5.01, 5.02, 5.03, 6.01,
6.02, 7, and 8. The Site is composed of an approximately 8.07-acre lot that has been developed
with three (3) structures: two (2) 1,200-sq-ft (sq-ft) structures and one (1) 350-sq-ft structure.

The objective of this ESA is to identify the presence or likely presence, use or release on the Site
of hazardous substances or petroleum products as defined in the American Standard of Testing
and Materials (ASTM) E1527-13 as a Recognized Environmental Condition (REC). In addition,
Business Environmental Risks (BERs), Controlled Recognized Environmental Conditions
(CRECs), and Historical Recognized Environmental Conditions (HRECs) were identified, if
present.

The following RECs have been identified:
•

REC 1: Historic Fill – A review of the EDR topographic Map Report and NJDEP
GEOWEB online mapping tool identified that historic fill is mapped at the Site. If future
development of the Site requires the excavation and off-Site disposal of soil, soil testing
and special handling of soil will be required. However, if future Site development does not
require excavation/disposal, soil may be left in place under a deed notice (soil Institutional
Control) and potential classification exception area (groundwater Institutional Control).

•

REC 2: Historical Railroad – Review of the EDR Historical Aerial Photograph Report
revealed evidence of a historical rail line at the Site. Residual components of the rail line
may remain at the Site. Some of these components may contain contaminants and should
be further evaluated. A sub-surface soil evaluation should be conducted to determine the
absence/presence of railroad components and/or resulting contamination.

•

REC 3: Historical Loading/Unloading Areas – Apparent loading and unloading areas
were observed on the EDR Historic Aerial Photograph Report in association with the
railroad. Additional sub-surface soil evaluation should be completed in locations where
loading/unloading may have occurred.

PHASE I ENVIRONMENTAL SITE ASSESSMENT REPORT
76 Central Avenue
Kearney, New Jersey

•

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 2 of 35

REC 4: Staining/Discharge – Evidence of petroleum discharge was observed in the
westernmost portion of the Site. Sub-surface soil evaluation is recommended.

•

REC 5: Exposed Chemical Storage – Several five (5)-gallon containers, partially filled
drums, and one (1) 250-gallon container were observed in the western portion of the Site,
situated directly on grass, gravel, or cracked asphalt. Furthermore, staining was observed
on some of the containers/drums, indicating that there is a potential for chemicals to have
been or to be released to the environment from these storage areas. Sub-surface soil
evaluation is recommended.

•

REC 6: Historical Spill – The Site was listed in the NJ Release database (Incident
Number 96-1-4-1350-29) on January 4, 1996 in relation to a reported release of an
unknown amount of paint from paint containers to the ground. Additional details regarding
this incident were not reported by EDR. Furthermore, the incident was not reported on
NJDEP’s online DataMiner database and the Site is not listed in the NJDEP Site
Remediation Program. Based on our Site evaluation, there was no evidence of the location
of the spill or NJDEP documentation that would indicate the spill was significant.
Therefore, no further evaluation is warranted.

The following CREC has been identified:
•

CREC 1: Groundwater Contamination – A review of the NJDEP GEOWEB identified a
Classification Exception Area (CEA) for contaminated groundwater that is mapped on an
adjacent property and extends onto this property. If future development requires
excavation and groundwater management activities, additional costs for sampling and
proper disposal may be required. No wells are listed as on the subject Site and testing will
be required to determine if groundwater has adversely impacted the Site from the
upgradient Site to the north.

The following BERs have been identified:
•

BER 2: Potential Contamination from Off-Site Sources – Due to the presence of known
contaminated properties, superfund sites, the contaminated Passaic River water body,
documented spills, and other commercial/industrial uses in the immediate vicinity of the
Site, there may be an influence on groundwater or soil vapor quality at the Site.

PHASE I ENVIRONMENTAL SITE ASSESSMENT REPORT
76 Central Avenue
Kearney, New Jersey

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 3 of 35

Furthermore, due to the tidally influenced nature of the Site, this concern is compounded
because water from immediate surrounding properties likely flow toward the Site. Please
note that if contamination is identified at the Site in connection with an off-Site source as
described by this BER, the User would not be the party responsible for the remediation.
However, the presence of contaminated groundwater may pose a risk to future building
occupants if there are vapor issues.
•

BER 3: Coastal Zone Management Regulation – Certain activities located at Sites within
the coastal zone are regulated by the NJDEP Division of Land Use Regulation. Depending
on the intended future use for the Site, the Site’s location within a coastal zone may restrict
development in those areas or will require NJDEP permits in order to conduct work
activities in these areas.

The RECs, BER, CREC, and HREC are discussed further in Section 10.0 CONCLUSIONS AND
RECOMMENDATIONS section of this Report.

2.0

INTRODUCTION

2.1

PURPOSE

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 4 of 35

The purpose of this study is to determine the RECs at the Site for due diligence purposes.

SESI has developed this Phase I ESA Report on behalf of the User. It has been developed in
substantial conformance with the ASTM’s International Standard Practice for Environmental Site
Assessments: Phase I Environmental Site Assessment Process E 1527-13. According to ASTM
E1527-13, the following conditions were identified during this assessment:
REC – “The presence or likely presence of any hazardous substances or petroleum products in,
on, or at the property: (1) due to release to the environments; (2) under conditions indicative of a
release to the environment; or (3) under conditions that pose a material threat of a future release
to the environment.”
CREC – “A recognized environmental condition resulting from a past release of hazardous
substances or petroleum products that has been addressed to the satisfaction of the applicable
regulatory authority.”
HREC – “A past release that has been remediated to below residential standards and given
regulatory closure with no use restrictions.”
BER – “A risk which can have a material environmental or environmentally-driven impact on the
business associated with the current or planned use of commercial real estate and is not an issue
required to be investigated under this practice.”

2.2

SCOPE OF SERVICES

The scope of this assessment consisted of the following general tasks: a records review; a Site
visit; interviews with present and past owners, operators, and occupants to the extent possible;
and preparation of this ESA report. Specific tasks that were conducted included the following:
•

A review of environmental databases maintained by the United States Environmental
Protection Agency (USEPA) and state and local agencies within the minimum search
distance. Environmental Data Resources, Inc. (EDR) prepares environmental databases
used in these assessments.

•

A Site reconnaissance to observe conditions and to assess the Site location with respect
to adjoining and surrounding property uses, types of development, topography, natural
surface features, roadways, railroad lines and right-of-ways to identify obvious potential
environmental conditions on neighboring properties.

PHASE I ENVIRONMENTAL SITE ASSESSMENT REPORT
76 Central Avenue
Kearney, New Jersey

•

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 5 of 35

Per ASTM E1527-13, questionnaires were provided to the User (Client), and Owner to
obtain information related to the Site.

2.3

SIGNIFICANT ASSUMPTIONS

Information obtained by SESI and by third parties is assumed to be accurate and/or true. SESI
makes no warranty, either implied or expressed, regarding the accuracy or validity of information
supplied by others.

2.4

LIMITATIONS/METHODOLOGY/RELIANCE

This ESA was prepared for the exclusive use of Criterion Group, LLC, its affiliates and lenders.
During the assessment, methodologies were used that conform to generally accepted practices
for this type of work. The scope and character of the environmental assessment were, in
professional judgment, sufficient to justify the stated conclusions, after giving due consideration
to the purpose of the investigation, the data reviewed, the background history of the Site as found
in historical records, and visual observation of the Site. No other investigation was performed
other than that which is expressly noted in this Report.

This ESA was performed in accordance with ASTM E1527‐13 and is subject to reasonable limits
of time and cost and is intended to reduce but not eliminate uncertainty regarding the potential for
RECs in connection with a property. Sampling and laboratory analysis are required to determine
if soil, surface water, groundwater, air, and construction materials have been impacted by a
contaminant.

The information presented in this ESA was obtained during the site reconnaissance and from the
Client, representatives of the Client, current/former property owner(s) or tenants, Federal, State,
County and Local Officials, and available mapped information obtained by SESI. SESI does not
certify the accuracy of the information received from outside sources.

This ESA should be used in its entirety. Use of portions of this ESA may nullify the information
and/or conclusions of this ESA. Site observations discussed herein apply to the Site conditions
as observed during Site reconnaissance only. The conclusions of this ESA do not apply to
conditions that were unavailable for inspection and could not be evaluated.

SESI does not certify that the Site is contaminant free and this ESA does not serve as a warranty.
SESI’s investigation was performed within the time frame requested by the client.

PHASE I ENVIRONMENTAL SITE ASSESSMENT REPORT
76 Central Avenue
Kearney, New Jersey

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 6 of 35

The activities to conduct this ESA did not include sampling and analyses of soil, surface water,
groundwater, air, or construction materials unless expressed otherwise and requested by the
client. Formal wetland, asbestos, mold, and/or radon evaluations were not performed and are not
within the scope of work for an ESA. If additional services including underground storage tank
(UST) testing, construction materials testing, or environmental sampling (soil, surface water,
groundwater, air) is recommended, the results and conclusions for these tests will be provided
under separate cover.

2.5

DISCLAIMER

This ESA was prepared by SESI for the sole and exclusive use of Criterion Group, LLC. Nothing
under the Agreement between SESI and the User shall be construed to give any rights or benefits
to anyone other than the User and SESI, and all duties and responsibilities undertaken pursuant
to the Agreement will be for the sole and exclusive benefit of the User and SESI and not for the
benefit of any other party. SESI does not intend, without its written consent, for this ESA to be
disseminated to anyone other than the User, or to be used or relied upon by anyone other than
the User. Use of the ESA by any other person is unauthorized and such use is at their sole risk.

3.0

SITE DESCRIPTION

3.1

LOCATION/STRUCTURES/LEGAL DESCRIPTION

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 7 of 35

The Site consists of an approximately 351,529-square feet (8.07-acre) parcel located at 76-84
Central Ave, Kearney, Hudson County, New Jersey. The Site is identified on tax map records as
Block 288; Lots 5.01, 5.02, 5.03, 6.01, 6.02, 7, and 8. A Site Location Plan is included on Figure
3.1. The Site is currently utilized as a rental car company and truck/vehicle storage lot. A trailer
is located in the eastern area of the property and houses the office space for the rental car
companies. Nearby, damaged rental vehicles are located in the eastern portion of the property.
An additional structure, that was inaccessible to SESI during the Site reconnaissance, is located
adjacent to the trailers. The western section of the Site contains tractor trailers. An existing Site
Plan is shown on Figure 3.2.

The Site is bound to the north by Distribution Avenue and an industrial property, to the west by
the Passaic River, to the south by a new commercial warehouse, and to the east by Central
Avenue and industrial/commercial lots. A summary of the uses of the adjacent properties is shown
on Table 3.1 below.
Table 3.1—Adjacent Property Uses
Direction

Adjacent Property Use

North

Distribution Avenue and industrial facility

South

Commercial warehouse facility

East

Central Avenue and industrial parking/storage

West

Passaic River

3.2

SITE & VICINITY CHARACTERISTICS

The Site is located in an industrial/commercial area between the confluence of the Passaic and
Hackensack Rivers. The closest notable surface water body is the Passaic River located
approximately 65 feet to the west of the site.

3.3

CURRENT USE OF THE PROPERTY

The Site is currently utilized for commercial and industrial purposes. The eastern portion of the
Site is occupied by rental car companies and the western portion of the property is utilized trucking
storage. The Site is owned by Jacjon Associates, LP. The Site is primarily used as parking area
for trucks.

PHASE I ENVIRONMENTAL SITE ASSESSMENT REPORT
76 Central Avenue
Kearney, New Jersey

3.4

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 8 of 35

DESCRIPTION OF STRUCTURES, ROADS, AND SITE IMPROVEMENTS

The Site is a rectangle-shaped property of approximately 8.07 acres. There are three (3) smaller
structures covering an estimated 0.07 acres of the Site. All areas are covered with gravel and
limited asphalt.

3.5

CURRENT USES OF ADJOINING PROPERTIES

The adjacent facilities provide warehouse storage, industrial manufacturing, and vehicle storage.

SPECIALIZED KNOWLEDGE/OTHER USER PROVIDED INFORMATION

4.3.1 INTERVIEWS
An environmental professional from SESI, Mr. Jeffrey Lamborn, conducted interviews with
operators of the subject Site in an effort to acquire pertinent information relating to current and
historical Site conditions. These interviews are summarized below.

Company Name: ELRAC, Inc (rental car company)
Date and Time of Interview: July 5, 2021
Type of Interview: In-person

Phase I ESA Site inspection and provided him with SESI’s Environmental Questionnaire. Mr. Don

Percora directed a tour of the facility. Mr. Pecora identified the eastern areas of the Site that were
previously covered by train tracks and identified four (4) conex boxes on the property currently
being leased by ELRAC, Inc (rental car company). Additional information obtained from this
interview, questionnaire and Site inspection is integrated into this ESA.

Name: Raphael
Company Name: (trucking company)
Date and Time of Interview: July 5, 2021
Type of Interview: In-person
SESI met with Raphael at 76 Central Ave, Kearney, New Jersey on July 5, 2021 for the Phase I
ESA Site inspection. Raphael directed a tour of the facility. Raphael provided access to the locked
area at the center of the west side, which is part of the trucking storage area. Raphael noted that
there was no heating source for the domed building and that the building was locked and
contained vehicles. Additional structures were identified as inaccessible (Figure 3.2). Additional
information obtained from this interview, questionnaire, and Site inspection is integrated in this
ESA.

Site photographs are included in a photographic log included in Appendix C.

4.4

COMMONLY KNOWN OR REASONABLY ASCERTAINABLE RECORDS

SESI completed a review of publicly available NJDEP mapping and database systems and the
United States Environmental Protection Agency (USEPA) database system as part of this Report
to determine the environmental conditions at the Site and adjacent properties. According to the
NJDEP database, no environmental sites of concern were identified. The database information is
attached to this Report as Appendix B. Based on our review, it was determined that the off-Site
superfund sites listed in NPL and NJEMS contained operations that have likely impacted the soil
and groundwater of the Site. In addition, historical use of the Site for railroad operations
contributed to the likelihood of contaminated urban fill. Additional investigation of the Site is
warranted to assess subsurface soil conditions, historic railroad contamination, and potential
migration off-Site.

Not requested at this search distance.

The EDR Radius Map Report identified operations at the Site in the following listings: NJEMS, NJ
Release and FINDS.

A discussion of these listings is provided below:

NJ Environmental Management System (NJEMS)
NJEMS are facilities that are of interest and are registered with the NJDEP under regulatory
permitting or enforcement programs. The Site is listed in the NJEMS database under the name
“Sea Box Inc”. According to NJDEP’s online database DataMiner, the Site is registered in the
NJDEP Right-to-Know (PI #60675100000) and Hazardous Waste (PI #NJX000312389)
programs. No violations or enforcement actions were identified in association with the facility.

NJ Release
The NJ Release database maintains a list of facilities where hazardous material release
conditions were reported to the NJDEP spill hotline without further investigation to determine their
validity or accuracy.
The subject Site was listed in the NJ Release database (Incident Number 96-1-4-1350-29) on
January 4, 1996 in relation to a reported release of an unknown amount of paint from paint
containers to the ground. Additional details regarding this incident were not reported by EDR.
Furthermore, the incident was not reported on NJDEP’s online DataMiner database and the Site
is not listed in the NJDEP Site Remediation Program. Based on the lack of information regarding
this incident, there is a potential for contamination to be present in relation to this listing. Therefore,
this spill is considered a REC.

PHASE I ENVIRONMENTAL SITE ASSESSMENT REPORT
76 Central Avenue
Kearney, New Jersey

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 14 of 35

Facility Index System/Facility Registry System (FINDS)
The FINDS database provides a list of facilities that maintain or previously maintained
environmental permits or are listed under additional EDR report databases. A review of the FINDS
list provided by EDR identified one (1) FINDS record for the Site, for Sea Box Inc. As previously
discussed, there was the potential for releases to occur during loading/unloading of hazardous
materials.

The EDR Radius Map Report identified operations at nearby facilities in the following listings:
NPL, SEMS, SEMS-ARCHIVE, CORRACTS, RCRA-TSDF, RCRA-LQG, RCRA-SQG, RCRA,
VSQG, US ENG CONTROLS, US INST CONTROLS, NJ SHWS, NJ HWS RE-EVAL, NJ LUST,
NJ HIST LUST, NJ UST, NJ ENG CONTROLS, NJ INST CONTROLS, NJ VCP, NJ
BROWNFIELDS, NJ Hist LF, RCRA Non-Gen/NLR, FUDS, ROD, CONSENT, NJ PF, NJ ISRA,
NJ MANIFEST and EDR MGP.

A discussion of these listings is provided below:

National Priority List (NPL) Sites
The EDR National Priority List (NPL) provides a list of Superfund facilities that are considered
priority cleanup facilities. One (1) Federal NPL facility is reported to be present within 0.125 miles
of the subject Site. The NPL site, known as the Diamond Alkali Superfund site, impacted the
Passaic River and some of the Hackensack River. The facility discharge area is located one foot
from the subject Site and the facility is currently on the final NPL.
Federal CERCLIS – Superfund Enterprise Management System (SEMS) Sites
According to the SEMS list provided by EDR, there are three (3) SEMS sites located within 0.5
miles of the subject Site: Diamond Alkali, Lucent Technologies (Western Electric), and Boasso
America Corporation. Based on the Site being adjacent to the Passaic River, the Diamond Alkali
facility has the potential to impact the subject Site. The Boasso America Corporation is
downgradient to the Site and adjacent the Passaic River; however, the area is likely tidally
influenced, so groundwater from the property has the potential to reach the Site. All Toxic
Substances Control Act (TSCA) and removal work is noted to be within the Site and no active
spills are listed. However, based on the active status of the SRP cases associated with the
property and the tidally influenced area, there is a potential environmental contamination from this
adjacent facility to impact the Site. Lucent Technologies (former AT&T Technologies facility) is

PHASE I ENVIRONMENTAL SITE ASSESSMENT REPORT
76 Central Avenue
Kearney, New Jersey

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 15 of 35

noted to contain lead contamination. No spills of this hazardous material are listed; however, the
facility is 745 feet upgradient of the Site and, therefore, could adversely impact the Site.
Furthermore, there are groundwater contamination areas to the north, south, and northeast of the
Site, with three (3) noted active Site remediations on the facility located at Block 288, Lot 10.01,
per NJ-GeoWeb.

Superfund Enterprise Management System Archive (SEMS-Archive)
SEMS-Archive a federal list that tracks hazardous, potentially hazardous, and remedial action
sites associated with the USEPA Superfund Program. Following remedial investigation at the
listed SEMS-Archive facilities, the USEPA determined that no additional assessment and/or
remediation will be completed under the Superfund Program. Therefore, these sites were
removed from the list. According to the SEMS list provided by EDR, there are two (2) facilities
(BASF Wyandotte Corp. and Veckridge Chemical Company) located within approximately 0.125
miles of the Site. These facilities are located southwest and downgradient from the Site and is not
expected to adversely impact the Site; however, based on the tidal conditions of the area, there
is a potential for the Site to be impacted by these two (2) facilities.

RCRA CORRACTS (CORRACTS)
The CORRACTS list identifies Resource Conservation and Recovery Action (RCRA) facilities that
incurred corrective action activity associated with their operation. According to the SEMS list
provided by EDR, there are nine (9) CORRACTS facilities within one (1) mile of the Site. As
previously discussed, this these facilities contain localized spills that are downgradient from the
Site or are part of an overall site remediation through the Superfund program that have active
remediation that may adversely impact the Site.
RCRA – Treatment, Storage, and Disposal Facilities (RCRA-TSDF)
The RCRA-TSDF includes a list of facilities that generate, transport, store, and/or dispose of
hazardous waste. According to the RCRA-TSDF list provided by EDR, there are seven (7) RCRATSDF facilities within approximately 0.5 miles of the Site. However, only three (3) of these facilities
are located on the same side of the Passaic River. These facilities are the BASF Wyandotte Corp.,
TP Industrial, Inc. and Lucent Technologies (Western Electric). Known hazardous waste and
contaminated groundwater is present on the Lucent Technologies parcel due to active site
remediation. The other facilities noted spills; however, these were contained, and the facilities are
downgradient from the subject Site.

PHASE I ENVIRONMENTAL SITE ASSESSMENT REPORT
76 Central Avenue
Kearney, New Jersey

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 16 of 35

RCRA Large Quantity Generator (RCRA-LQG)
The RCRA-LQG includes a list of facilities that generate over 1,000 kilograms of hazardous waste,
or over 1 kilogram of acutely hazardous waste per month. According to the RCRA-LQG list
provided by EDR, there is one (1) RCRA-LQG facility (Elan Chemical Co. Inc.) within
approximately 0.25 miles of the Site. Based on the distance from the Site to the facility sources
and groundwater/surface water flow direction, this facility is not expected to adversely impact the
Site.

RCRA Small Quantity Generator (RCRA-SQG)
The RCRA-SQG includes a list of facilities that generate more than 100 but less than 1,000
kilograms of hazardous waste per month and accumulate less than 6,000 kilograms of hazardous
waste at any time. According to the RCRA-SQG list provided by EDR, there are three (3) RCRASQG facilities (Amazon.com Services LLC, Hudson County/Bldg 77 Warehouse, and River
Terminal Development) within approximately 0.25 miles of the Site. A review of the facilities did
not identify any environmental release(s) of hazardous materials. Therefore, these facilities are
not expected to adversely impact the Site.

RCRA Very Small Quantity Generator (RCRA-VSQG)
The RCRA-VSQG includes a list of facilities that generate less than 100 kilograms of hazardous
waste, or less than 1 kilogram of acutely hazardous waste per month. According to the RCRAVSQG list provided by EDR, there are three (3) RCRA-VSQG facilities within approximately 0.25
miles of the Site. The National Distillers & Chemical Corp. is located to the west of the Site across
the Passaic River and is not expected to adversely impact the Site. The Diamond Alkali Superfund
facility is identified to have impacted a stretch of the Passaic River, and, therefore, is considered
to have impacted the Site, which abuts the river to the west. This Superfund facility is discussed
previously and listed as a CREC. The third facility is the BASF facility located downgradient from
the Site. That facility released small quantities of material as a NJ spill. The spill and remediation
area are located downgradient to the Site; however, based on the likely tidal influence on this
area; there is a potential for this facility to impact the Site.

US Engineering Controls (US ENG CONTROL)
The US ENG CONTROL list identifies facilities that utilize caps, building foundations, liners and/or
similar methods to protect human health and the environment. According to the US ENG
CONTROLS list provided by EDR, there is one (1) US ENG CONTROLS facility within

PHASE I ENVIRONMENTAL SITE ASSESSMENT REPORT
76 Central Avenue
Kearney, New Jersey

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 17 of 35

approximately 0.125 miles of the Site. As previously discussed, this facility is expected to
adversely impact the Site.

US Institutional Controls (US INST CONTROLS)
The US INST CONTROLS list identifies facilities that utilize administrative restrictions to
groundwater use, construction, property use and post-remediation care requirements to prevent
exposure to remaining contamination. According to the US INST CONTROL list provided by EDR,
there is one (1) US INST CONTROL facility within approximately 0.125 miles of the Site. As
previously discussed, the contaminated sediment from this facility is expected to adversely impact
the Site.

NJ State Hazardous Waste Sites (NJ SHWS)
NJ SHWS is a list of known contaminated sites in New Jersey except those associated with
Bureau of Underground Storage Sites (BUST). According to the NJ SHWS list provided by EDR,
there are one hundred thirty-six (136) SHWS facilities located within approximately one (1) mile
of the Site. Remediation on one hundred and five (105) facilities has been completed, while the
other facilities are actively being remediated. The nearest active facilities are BASF Corp at 50
Central Ave and TP Industrial, Inc. at 49 Central Ave. Both facilities are located directly
downgradient of the Site; however, based on the proximity and likely tidal influence on this area,
the facilities have the potential to impact the environment at the Site.

The active River Terminal Development facility at 100 Central Ave is to the north and upgradient
of the Site. This Site is associated with the Western Electric Company and is listed as an REC
due to the active remediation upgradient of the subject Site. The remedial action is planned to be
completed for all areas of concern by June 18, 2026.

The Hudson County USS Juneau Memorial Center is also listed as an active facility at 110 South
Hackensack Ave approximately 0.232 miles to the northeast of the subject Site. The Site contains
Engineering Controls and the next remedial document to be received by the NJDEP is a Remedial
Action Protectiveness/Biennial Certification Form, due on May 4, 2022. This facility does not show
the potential for groundwater to adversely impact the subject Site.

PHASE I ENVIRONMENTAL SITE ASSESSMENT REPORT
76 Central Avenue
Kearney, New Jersey

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 18 of 35

NJ State Hazardous Waste Sites Re-Evaluation (NJ HWS RE-EVAL)
The RE-EVAL facilities are locations that were removed from the Known Contaminated Sites list
and that the NJDEP is now re-evaluating. According to the NJ HWS RE-EVAL list provided by
EDR, there are nine (9) HWS RE-EVAL facilities located within approximately 1 mile of the Site.
Based on the current status and their apparent down-gradient/side-gradient location, these NJ
HWS Re-EVAL facilities are not expected to adversely impact the Site.

NJ Leaking Underground Storage Tanks (NJ LUST) and NJ HIST LUST
The NJ LUST is a listing of regulated Underground Storage Tanks that have a cleanup underway.
The NJ HIST LUST is a listing of historic USTs that are no longer updated or maintained by the
NJDEP. A review of the NJ LUST list provided by EDR has revealed that there are eleven (11)
NJ LUST facilities and ten (10) NJ HIST LUST facilities within approximately 0.5 miles of the
subject Site. Most of these facilities previously received No Further Action (NFA) letters from the
NJDEP or have installed Engineering Controls. Based on the anticipated groundwater flow
direction and the distance to the subject Site, these facilities are not expected to adversely impact
the Site.

NJ Underground Storage Tanks (NJ UST)
The NJ Underground Storage Tank list collates all underground storage tanks that were reported
to the NJDEP. There are nine (9) listings of facilities noted within 0.25 miles of the Site that contain
UST(s). Seven (7) of these listings are on the same side of the Passaic River as the subject Site.
The tanks have been removed or abandoned in place at all but one of these seven facilities. The
exception is the River Terminal Development which is considered duplicate. Since there are no
reported spills, it is unlikely that this facility would have adversely impacted the Site.

NJ Engineering Controls (NJ ENG CONTROL)
The NJ ENG CONTROL list identifies facilities that utilize caps, building foundations, liners and/or
similar methods to protect human health and the environment. According to the NJ ENG
CONTROLS list provided by EDR, there is one (1) facility is downgradient the subject Site and
another facility to the northeast of the Site, contains an asphalt cap due to the release of petroleum
products. This facility is not expected to have adversely impacted the subject Site as there is no
documentation that suggests contamination from this facility from the northeast. However, further
investigation on-Site may provide additional insight into possible contamination from these
petroleum products from this facility.

PHASE I ENVIRONMENTAL SITE ASSESSMENT REPORT
76 Central Avenue
Kearney, New Jersey

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 19 of 35

NJ Institutional Controls (NJ INST CONTROLS)
The NJ INST CONTROLS list identifies facilities that utilize administrative restrictions to
groundwater use, construction, property use and post-remediation care requirements to prevent
exposure to remaining contamination. According to the NJ INST CONTROL list provided by EDR,
there are three (3) NJ INST CONTROL facilities within approximately 0.25 miles of the Site.
Known CEAs are listed for 100 Central Ave for various compounds and 110 S Hackensack Ave
for metals. As previously discussed, the CEA for 100 Central Ave extends into the Site. The CEA
for 110 S Hackensack does not extend to the Site and is not expected to adversely impact the
subject Site.

NJ Voluntary Clean Up Program (NJ VCP)
The NJ VCP is a list of responsible parties, developers, local officials, or individuals working with
the department to remediate non-priority contaminated sites that pose no immediate threat to
human health or the environment. A review of NJ VCP list provided by EDR has identified four (4)
facilities with five (5) listings in the NJ VCP database. Two of these listings are located west of
the Site on the other side of the Passaic River and are not expected to adversely impact the Site.
The three (3) other listings are from facilities noted as historical, and those facilities are unlikely
to affect the Site.

NJ BROWNFIELDS
NJ BROWNFIELDS are facilities that are identified as former or current commercial or industrial
use facilities that are presently vacant or underutilized, on which there is suspected to have been
a discharge of contamination to the soil or groundwater at concentrations greater than applicable
cleanup criteria. A review of NJ BROWNFIELDS list provided by EDR has revealed that there
are fifteen (15) NJ BROWNFIELD facilities within approximately 0.5 miles of the subject Site.
Based on our review of available information and mapping for these facilities, the following was
concluded: groundwater contamination extends into the Site from the north (per NJ-GeoWeb) and
historic fill is noted to be used on all surrounding properties (likely the Site as well). Groundwater
flow is anticipated to be towards the south, and soil contamination may extend from the northern
former Western Electric facility to the subject Site.

RCRA Non-Generators/No Longer Regulated (RCRA NonGen/NLR)
Resource Conservation and Recovery Act Non-Generators (RCRA NonGen) database searches
USEPA’s comprehensive information system, providing access to data supporting the RCRA of

PHASE I ENVIRONMENTAL SITE ASSESSMENT REPORT
76 Central Avenue
Kearney, New Jersey

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 20 of 35

1976 and the Hazardous and Solid Waste Amendments (HSWA) of 1984. The database includes
selective information about facilities that generate, transport, store, treat and/ or dispose of
hazardous waste as described by RCRA. Non-Generators do not presently generate hazardous
waste. A review of RCRA NonGen/NLR list provided by EDR has revealed that there are twentytwo (22) facilities within 0.25 miles of the subject Site. Since none of these facilities currently
generate hazardous waste, they are not expected to adversely impact the Site.

Formerly Used Defense Site (FUDS)
The FUDS list provides an inventory of historically operated defense facilities where the US Army
Corps of Engineers is working or will take necessary cleanup actions. A review of the FUDS list,
as provided by EDR, has revealed that there are two (2) FUDS facilities within approximately one
(1) mile of the subject Site. A review of available NJDEP databases was conducted; however,
evidence of a release of hazardous materials was not identified. Therefore, these facilities do not
constitute RECs.

Record of Decision (ROD)
The ROD list includes facilities where a permanent remedy at an NPL Superfund has been
mandated. A review of the ROD list provided by EDR has revealed that there is one (1) facility
(i.e., Diamond Alkalis Superfund Site) within 0.125 miles of the subject Site. As previously
discussed, there is a potential for contamination from this facility to impact the Site.

CONSENT/ROD
The Consent/ROD list provides an inventory of facilities that have mandated and permanent
remedies in place and Superfund sites containing technical and health information to aid in the
cleanup. One (1) CONSENT/ROD facility, Diamond Alkali, is within 0.125 miles of the Site. As
previously discussed, there is a potential for contamination from this facility to impact the Site.

NJ Publicly Funded (NJ PF)
The NJ PF is a list of facilities where remediation costs are being funded by the NJDEP with public
funds. A review of NJ PF list provided by EDR has revealed that there is one (1) NJ PF facility
within approximately one (1) mile of the subject Site. Based on the distance to the subject Site,
this facility is not expected to have adversely impacted the Site.

PHASE I ENVIRONMENTAL SITE ASSESSMENT REPORT
76 Central Avenue
Kearney, New Jersey

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 21 of 35

NJ Industrial Site Recovery Act (ISRA)
NJ ISRA is a list of sites that are defined as an "industrial establishment” as specified in the NJ
ISRA. The place of business must have been engaged in operations on or after December 31,
1983 and must involve the generation, manufacture, refining, transportation, treatment, storage,
handling, or disposal of hazardous substances or hazardous wastes. A review of NJ ISRA list
provided by EDR has revealed that there are sixteen (16) NJ ISRA facilities within approximately
0.5 miles of the subject Site. Four (4) of these facilities are located on the same side of the Passaic
River as the subject Site. These facilities have either received No Further Action letters from
NJDEP or do not have documented contamination migrating towards the Site. One (1) facility
located to the north of the subject Site, 100 Central Ave, is listed as a BREC.

MANIFEST Sites
MANIFEST Sites include a list of facilities that have generated hazardous waste or contaminants.
A review of the NY, NJ, and PA MANIFEST list, as provided by EDR, has revealed that there are
five (5) NY MANIFEST, five (5) NJ MANIFEST facilities and one (1) PA MANIFEST facility within
approximately 0.25 miles of the subject Site. Based on a review of the available database
information, documented release events have not occurred at the upgradient facilities and
therefore do not constitute a REC.

EDR Manufactured Gas Plant (MGP) Sites
The MGP list provides an inventory of facilities that formerly operated coal gas plants. A review
of the EDR MGP database, as provided by EDR, has revealed that there is one (1) MGP facility
within approximately one (1) mile of the subject Site. This facility is located to the northeast of the
subject Site across the Passaic River. Therefore, this facility does not constitute a REC.

PREVIOUS ENVIRONMENTAL REPORTS

SESI was not provided with any previous environmental reports for the property.

UST decommissioning records. Information was not received nor have file reviews been
scheduled for this Site. The Town of Kearney noted that information will be gathered by August

5.2

PHYSICAL SETTING SOURCES

Sources used to evaluate the physical setting of the property include:
•
•
•

5.2.1

USGS Topographic Maps
Aerial Photographs
Site Reconnaissance

TOPOGRAPHY

The Site is relatively level with regional topography sloping down to the south. According to the
United States Geological Survey (USGS) Elizabeth and Jersey City, New Jersey, 2014, 7.5minute Series topographic maps, the Site’s topographic elevation is approximately 9 feet above
mean sea level (msl). The EDR Topographic Map Report is attached to this Report as Appendix
A.

5.2.2

SURFACE WATERS AND WETLANDS

The Site is located between the confluence of the Passaic and Hackensack Rivers. The closest
notable surface water body is the Passaic River located approximately 65 feet to the west of the
site.

5.2.3

GEOLOGY AND GROUNDWATER

As per the USDA National Cooperative Soil Survey (NCSS) map for the area, the soils at the Site
are characterized as URWEB (Urban Land), wet substratum, 0 to 8 percent slopes. The depth of
the groundwater at the Site is approximately nine (9) feet below ground surface (bgs), and
groundwater flow direction is expected to be in the south direction.

ENVIRONMENTAL LIEN AND AUL SEARCH REPORT

Based on data provided by EDR, no environmental liens nor any other activity or use limitations
were found.

5.4

ENVIRONMENTAL PERMITS

Review of the EDR Radius Map Report for the Site indicates no environmental permits attributed
to the Site. EDR Reports are provided in Appendix A. However, according to NJDEP DataMiner,
Bohler Engineering (located at 78-84 Central Avenue in Kearny, NJ) is identified as active in the
Coastal and Land Use program (Site ID #171327) with one (1) Land Use permit associated with
the property (Activity #APD 040001). The permit was issued on April 14, 2004 in relation to a land
use jurisdictional determination.

PHASE I ENVIRONMENTAL SITE ASSESSMENT REPORT
76 Central Avenue
Kearney, New Jersey

6.0

SITE RECONNAISSANCE

6.1

METHODOLOGY AND LIMITING CONDITIONS

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 27 of 35

An environmental professional from SESI, Jeff Lamborn, visited the Site on July 7 to identify
potential RECs. The Site was observed to the extent that it was accessible and not obstructed.
An inspection photolog is attached to this Report as Appendix C.

6.2

SITE OBSERVATIONS

The Site is improved by primarily gravel and some cracked concrete parking areas, a few small
covered structures, Conex boxes, cars, a two (2)-story office building, and parked trucks and
trailers. An abandoned rail line was also observed to the south and northeast of the property.

6.3

CHEMICAL STORAGE AND USAGE

A 250-gallon tote was present on-Site and filled with an unknown liquid.

6.4

BULK STORAGE TANKS

Damaged fuel tanks, gas cylinders, and truck parts were found in some areas of the property.

6.5

SITE WASTE AND WASTEWATER

One (1) Waste Management dumpster was present on Site and covered. The dumpster was
identified to be in good condition with no evidence of release (i.e., staining, discoloration), within
its vicinity. The Site generally contained discarded residual waste and tires throughout the Site.

6.6

STAINED SOIL, STAINED PAVEMENT, OR STRESSED VEGETATION

SESI observed evidence of petroleum staining/discharge in the western portion of the Site.

6.7

PITS, PONDS, OR LAGOONS

No pits, ponds or lagoons were identified at the Site.

6.8

WELLS

SESI did not observe any on-Site dry wells, irrigation wells, injection wells, observation wells,
monitoring wells, potable water wells, recovery wells, or abandoned wells.

PHASE I ENVIRONMENTAL SITE ASSESSMENT REPORT
76 Central Avenue
Kearney, New Jersey

6.9

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 28 of 35

FLOOR DRAINS AND SUMPS

One (1) catch basin was observed in the rental car parking lot (west portion of the property). There
was no visible sheen or staining, nor discharge outlet.
6.10

PCBs

Two (2) pole-mounted transformers were observed along the north boundary of the property. No
staining or evidence of a release from these transformers was observed. No additional
investigation is warranted.

6.11

ASBESTOS-CONTAINING MATERIAL (ACM)

ACM surveying was not included in SESI’s scope of work; however, based on the age of the
facility, an ACM survey is recommended.

6.12

RADON

Radon surveying was not included in SESI’s scope of work; however, based on the age of the
facility, a Radon survey is recommended.

6.13

LEAD-BASED PAINT (LBP)

LBP surveying was not included in SESI’s scope of work; however, based on the age of the facility,
and LBP survey is recommended.

6.14

VAPOR INTRUSION

Based on a review of site-related information, vapor intrusion may be of concern due to the
presence of volatile organic compounds (VOCs) petroleum materials.

PHASE I ENVIRONMENTAL SITE ASSESSMENT REPORT
76 Central Avenue
Kearney, New Jersey

7.0

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 29 of 35

DATA GAPS

Based on a review of the available documentation, the following data gaps have been identified:
•

SESI was not provided access to a few shipping containers that may be used for storage of
chemicals or equipment. In addition, the buildings on the west side of the property were
inaccessible during the time of the walkthrough.

•

Truck/vehicle, equipment, and trailer storage encountered at the Site prevented unhindered
observation of the ground surface.

•

No previous environmental documents were provided for this review.

•

The questionnaire provided to the tenant of the property has not yet been returned.

•

An OPRA request was made to municipal, county and state agencies. Document searches
will be conducted after a response is provided.

If additional information or documents show to have adversely impact the Site, SESI will alter this
Report to reflect any new findings.

FINDINGS

Based on our investigation, we conclude there is evidence to indicate areas of potential
environmental concern on Site due to the historical usage as a vehicle repair and storage facility
as well as a historical railroad intersection for transport of goods/people across the Passaic River.
See Section 10.0 CONCLUSIONS of this Report.

OPINION

In our professional judgment, the scope and character of our environmental assessment were
sufficient to justify the stated conclusions, after giving due consideration to the purpose of the
investigation, the data reviewed, the background history of the Site as found in historical records,
and visual observation of the Site.

Page 32 of 35

CONCLUSIONS AND RECOMMENDATIONS

Based on our investigation, we conclude that there is evidence to indicate areas of REC on the
Site and that additional investigation of these areas is warranted. The RECs are depicted on
Figure 3.3, the REC Location Plan. Our conclusions and recommendations in this regard are as
follows:
•

REC 1: Historic Fill – A review of the EDR topographic Map Report and NJDEP
GEOWEB online mapping tool identified that historic fill is mapped at the Site. If future
development of the Site requires the excavation and off-Site disposal of soil, soil testing
and special handling of soil will be required. However, if future Site development does not
require excavation/disposal, soil may be left in place under a deed notice (soil Institutional
Control) and potential classification exception area (groundwater Institutional Control).

•

REC 2: Historical Railroad – Review of the EDR Historical Aerial Photograph Report
revealed evidence of a historical rail line at the Site. Residual components of the rail line
may remain at the Site. Some of these components may contain contaminants and should
be further evaluated. A sub-surface soil evaluation should be conducted to determine the
absence/presence of railroad components and/or resulting contamination.

•

REC 3: Historical Loading/Unloading Areas – Apparent loading and unloading areas
were observed on the EDR Historic Aerial Photograph Report in association with the
railroad. Additional sub-surface soil evaluation should be completed in locations where
loading/unloading may have occurred.

•

REC 4: Staining/Discharge – Evidence of petroleum discharge was observed in the
westernmost portion of the Site. Sub-surface soil evaluation is recommended.

•

REC 5: Exposed Chemical Storage – Several five (5)-gallon containers, partially filled
drums, and one (1) 250-gallon container were observed in the western portion of the Site,
situated directly on grass, gravel, or cracked asphalt. Furthermore, staining was observed
on some of the containers/drums, indicating that there is a potential for chemicals to have
been or to be released to the environment from these storage areas. Sub-surface soil
evaluation is recommended.

PHASE I ENVIRONMENTAL SITE ASSESSMENT REPORT
76 Central Avenue
Kearney, New Jersey

•

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 33 of 35

REC 6: Historical Spill – The Site was listed in the NJ Release database (Incident
Number 96-1-4-1350-29) on January 4, 1996 in relation to a reported release of an
unknown amount of paint from paint containers to the ground. Additional details regarding
this incident were not reported by EDR. Furthermore, the incident was not reported on
NJDEP’s online DataMiner database and the Site is not listed in the NJDEP Site
Remediation Program. Based on our Site evaluation, there was no evidence of the location
of the spill or NJDEP documentation that would indicate the spill was significant.
Therefore, no further evaluation is warranted.

Additionally, two one (1) CREC and two (2) BERs were identified:
•

CREC 1: Groundwater Contamination – A review of the NJDEP GEOWEB identified a
Classification Exception Area (CEA) for contaminated groundwater that is mapped on an
adjacent property and extends onto this property. If future development requires
excavation and groundwater management activities, additional costs for sampling and
proper disposal may be required. No wells are listed as on the subject Site and testing will
be required to determine if groundwater has adversely impacted the Site from the
upgradient Site to the north.

•

BER 2: Potential Contamination from Off-Site Sources – Due to the presence of known
contaminated properties, superfund sites, the contaminated Passaic River water body,
documented spills, and other commercial/industrial uses in the immediate vicinity of the
Site, there may be an influence on groundwater or soil vapor quality at the Site.
Furthermore, due to the tidally influenced nature of the Site, this concern is compounded
because water from immediate surrounding properties likely flow toward the Site. Please
note that if contamination is identified at the Site in connection with an off-Site source as
described by this BER, the User would not be the party responsible for the remediation.
However, the presence of contaminated groundwater may pose a risk to future building
occupants if there are vapor issues.

•

BER 3: Coastal Zone Management Regulation – Certain activities located at Sites within
the coastal zone are regulated by the NJDEP Division of Land Use Regulation. Depending
on the intended future use for the Site, the Site’s location within a coastal zone may restrict
development in those areas or will require NJDEP permits in order to conduct work
activities in these areas.

PHASE I ENVIRONMENTAL SITE ASSESSMENT REPORT
76 Central Avenue
Kearney, New Jersey

•

August 2021
SESI Project. No. 11975:1
Page 34 of 35

BER 3: Potential Former Use of Heating Oil – Based on historical aerial photographs,
there were likely additional former structure(s) present at the Site that may have utilized
AST(s) or UST(s) containing heating oil. Neither the size nor construction of the former
potential AST(s) or UST(s) is known. SESI is unable to rule out the potential presence of
AST(s) and/or UST(s) associated with the former on-Site structures at the Site.

The Sanborn Library has been searched by EDR and maps covering the target property location as provided by SESI Consulting Engineers
were identified for the years listed below. The Sanborn Library is the largest, most complete collection of fire insurance maps. The collection
includes maps from Sanborn, Bromley, Perris & Browne, Hopkins, Barlow, and others. Only Environmental Data Resources Inc. (EDR) is
authorized to grant rights for commercial reproduction of maps by the Sanborn Library LLC, the copyright holder for the collection. Results
can be authenticated by visiting www.edrnet.com/sanborn.
The Sanborn Library is continually enhanced with newly identified map archives. This report accesses all maps in the collection as of the
day this report was generated.

The EDR Environmental Lien Search Report provides results from a search of available current land title records
for environmental cleanup liens and other activity and use limitations, such as engineering controls and
institutional controls.
A network of professional, trained researchers, following established procedures, uses client supplied address
information to:
• search for parcel information and/or legal description;
•
search for ownership information;
•
research official land title documents recorded at jurisdictional agencies such as recorders' offices,
registries of deeds, county clerks' offices, etc.;
•
access a copy of the deed;
•
search for environmental encumbering instrument(s) associated with the deed;
•
provide a copy of any environmental encumbrance(s) based upon a review of key words in the
instrument(s) (title, parties involved, and description); and
•
provide a copy of the deed or cite documents reviewed.

Thank you for your business.
Please contact EDR at 1-800-352-0050
with any questions or comments.

environmental professionals in evaluating potential liability on a target property resulting from past activities. EDR’s
professional researchers provide digitally reproduced historical aerial photographs, and when available, provide one photo
per decade.

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

DESCRIPTION
Environmental Data Resources, Inc.’s (EDR) City Directory Abstract is a screening tool designed to assist
environmental professionals in evaluating potential liability on a target property resulting from past activities.
EDR’s City Directory Abstract includes a search and abstract of available city directory data. For each
address, the directory lists the name of the corresponding occupant at five year intervals.
Business directories including city, cross reference and telephone directories were reviewed, if available, at
approximately five year intervals for the years spanning 1923 through 2017. This report compiles
information gathered in this review by geocoding the latitude and longitude of properties identified and
gathering information about properties within 660 feet of the target property.
A summary of the information obtained is provided in the text of this report.
RECORD SOURCES
EDR's Digital Archive combines historical directory listings from sources such as Cole Information and Dun
& Bradstreet. These standard sources of property information complement and enhance each other to
provide a more comprehensive report.
EDR is licensed to reproduce certain City Directory works by the copyright holders of those works. The
purchaser of this EDR City Directory Report may include it in report(s) delivered to a customer. Reproduction
of City Directories without permission of the publisher or licensed vendor may be a violation of copyright.

SURROUNDING SITES: SEARCH RESULTS

Surrounding sites were identified in the following databases.
Elevations have been determined from the USGS Digital Elevation Model and should be evaluated on
a relative (not an absolute) basis. Relative elevation information between sites of close proximity
should be field verified. Sites with an elevation equal to or higher than the target property have been
differentiated below from sites with an elevation lower than the target property.
Page numbers and map identification numbers refer to the EDR Radius Map report where detailed
data on individual sites can be reviewed.
Sites listed in bold italics are in multiple databases.
Unmappable (orphan) sites are not considered in the foregoing analysis.

STANDARD ENVIRONMENTAL RECORDS

Federal NPL site list
NPL: Also known as Superfund, the National Priority List database is a subset of CERCLIS and
identifies over 1,200 sites for priority cleanup under the Superfund program. The source of this database is
the U.S. EPA.
A review of the NPL list, as provided by EDR, and dated 04/27/2021 has revealed that there is 1 NPL
site within approximately 1 mile of the target property.

11

Federal CERCLIS list
SEMS: SEMS (Superfund Enterprise Management System) tracks hazardous waste sites, potentially
hazardous waste sites, and remedial activities performed in support of EPA’s Superfund Program across the
United States. The list was formerly know as CERCLIS, renamed to SEMS by the EPA in 2015. The list contains
data on potentially hazardous waste sites that have been reported to the USEPA by states, municipalities,
private companies and private persons, pursuant to Section 103 of the Comprehensive Environmental Response,
Compensation, and Liability Act (CERCLA). This dataset also contains sites which are either proposed to or on
the National Priorities List (NPL) and the sites which are in the screening and assessment phase for possible
inclusion on the NPL.
A review of the SEMS list, as provided by EDR, and dated 04/27/2021 has revealed that there are 3
SEMS sites within approximately 0.5 miles of the target property.

Federal CERCLIS NFRAP site list
SEMS-ARCHIVE: SEMS-ARCHIVE (Superfund Enterprise Management System Archive) tracks sites that have no
further interest under the Federal Superfund Program based on available information. The list was formerly
known as the CERCLIS-NFRAP, renamed to SEMS ARCHIVE by the EPA in 2015. EPA may perform a minimal level of

TC6549419.2s EXECUTIVE SUMMARY 12

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
assessment work at a site while it is archived if site conditions change and/or new information becomes
available. Archived sites have been removed and archived from the inventory of SEMS sites. Archived status
indicates that, to the best of EPA’s knowledge, assessment at a site has been completed and that EPA has
determined no further steps will be taken to list the site on the National Priorities List (NPL), unless
information indicates this decision was not appropriate or other considerations require a recommendation for
listing at a later time. The decision does not necessarily mean that there is no hazard associated with a
given site; it only means that. based upon available information, the location is not judged to be potential
NPL site.
A review of the SEMS-ARCHIVE list, as provided by EDR, and dated 04/27/2021 has revealed that there
are 8 SEMS-ARCHIVE sites within approximately 0.5 miles of the target property.

Federal RCRA non-CORRACTS TSD facilities list
RCRA-TSDF: RCRAInfo is EPA’s comprehensive information system, providing access to data supporting
the Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA) of 1976 and the Hazardous and Solid Waste Amendments (HSWA)
of 1984. The database includes selective information on sites which generate, transport, store, treat and/or
dispose of hazardous waste as defined by the Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA). Transporters are
individuals or entities that move hazardous waste from the generator offsite to a facility that can recycle,
treat, store, or dispose of the waste. TSDFs treat, store, or dispose of the waste.
A review of the RCRA-TSDF list, as provided by EDR, and dated 03/22/2021 has revealed that there are
7 RCRA-TSDF sites within approximately 0.5 miles of the target property.

Federal RCRA generators list
RCRA-LQG: RCRAInfo is EPA’s comprehensive information system, providing access to data supporting
the Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA) of 1976 and the Hazardous and Solid Waste Amendments (HSWA)
of 1984. The database includes selective information on sites which generate, transport, store, treat and/or
dispose of hazardous waste as defined by the Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA). Large quantity
generators (LQGs) generate over 1,000 kilograms (kg) of hazardous waste, or over 1 kg of acutely hazardous
waste per month.
A review of the RCRA-LQG list, as provided by EDR, and dated 03/22/2021 has revealed that there is 1

US INST CONTROLS: A listing of sites with institutional controls in place. Institutional controls include
administrative measures, such as groundwater use restrictions, construction restrictions, property use
restrictions, and post remediation care requirements intended to prevent exposure to contaminants remaining on
site. Deed restrictions are generally required as part of the institutional controls.

2047

NJ ISRA: The ISRA process begins with determining if the Act applies to your type of business and
transaction. The provisions of ISRA only apply to industrial establishments. What is an industrial
establishment? The term "industrial establishment" refers to the type of business operations and transactions
that would subject a facility to review under ISRA. An industrial establishment must meet each of the
following three criteria: The place of business or real property at which such business is conducted, having
a North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code listed in N.J.A.C. 7:26 B - Appendix C subject to
the specified exceptions and limitations. The place of business must have been engaged in operations on or
after December 31, 1983; and The place of business must involve the generation, manufacture, refining,
transportation, treatment, storage, handling, or disposal of hazardous substances or hazardous wastes.
A review of the NJ ISRA list, as provided by EDR, and dated 03/29/2021 has revealed that there are 16
NJ ISRA sites within approximately 0.5 miles of the target property.

EDR HIGH RISK HISTORICAL RECORDS

EDR Exclusive Records
EDR MGP: The EDR Proprietary Manufactured Gas Plant Database includes records of coal gas plants
(manufactured gas plants) compiled by EDR’s researchers. Manufactured gas sites were used in the United States
from the 1800’s to 1950’s to produce a gas that could be distributed and used as fuel. These plants used
whale oil, rosin, coal, or a mixture of coal, oil, and water that also produced a significant amount of waste.
Many of the byproducts of the gas production, such as coal tar (oily waste containing volatile and
non-volatile chemicals), sludges, oils and other compounds are potentially hazardous to human health and the
environment. The byproduct from this process was frequently disposed of directly at the plant site and can
remain or spread slowly, serving as a continuous source of soil and groundwater contamination.
A review of the EDR MGP list, as provided by EDR, has revealed that there is 1 EDR MGP site within
approximately 1 mile of the target property.

Database(s)

DIAMOND ALKALI SUPERFUND SITE (Continued)

EDR ID Number
EPA ID Number

1000301205

Summary Details:
Conditions at listing September 1983): The Diamond Alkali Co. Site
occupies about l acre immediately adjacent to the Passaic River, in Newark,
Essex County, New Jersey. From 1943 through l968, the company manufactured
numerous chemicals onthe site, including 2,4,5-trichlorophenol, which is
likely to contain dioxin as an impurity. Extensive sampling conducted by EPA
and the State has detected extremely high concentrations of dioxin on and off
the site. EPA and the State have covered the area of major contamination with
plastic tarpaulins. Adjacent transportation routes and residential areas
were swept and vacuumed. Workers may have been exposed to dioxin during
normal operations, as well as during renovations conducted during the summer
of l982. Another company has since purchased the land. The area is
both densely populated and heavily industriali ed. Municipal water is drawn
from the Wanaque Reservoir, roughly 35 miles from the site. Status June
1984): On March 13, 1984, the State and Diamond entered into an
Administrative Order on Consent for a remedial investigation/feasibility study
and for on-site cleanup.
